steps:
1. Full volume of tank = length × width × height = 80 cm × 40 cm × 50 cm.
2. Calculate: 80 × 40 = 3200 cm² (base area).
3. Multiply by height: 3200 × 50 = 160,000 cm³ (full volume).
4. Water fills to three-quarters of height, so water height = (3/4) × 50 cm = 37.5 cm.
5. Volume of water = base area × water height = 3200 cm² × 37.5 cm.
6. 3200 × 37.5 = 3200 × (75/2) = (3200 × 75) / 2 = (240,000) / 2 = 120,000 cm³.
7. Check options: 120,000 cm³ matches option A.
